clarified_problem: Improve the current URL shortening service to include expiring links, enhanced analytics, while maintaining backward compatibility.
assumptions: The existing URL shortener operates on a POST request and returns a shortened URL.
Existing redirects are based on GET requests and should not be affected by the changes.

tasks.title: LLM Task 1
tasks.description: Implement an expiring link feature for each shortened URL, allowing users to specify the expiration time.

ai_assist.prompt: Refine this task into implementation steps: Implement an expiring link feature for each shortened URL, allowing users to specify the expiration time.
ai_assist.expected_ai_output: Draft implementation artifacts that require engineer review before acceptance.

tasks.engineer_validation: Confirm the task output is technically correct, scoped, and testable.

tasks.title: LLM Task 2
tasks.description: Develop analytics tracking that provides detailed information about the usage of the shortened URLs.

ai_assist.prompt: Refine this task into implementation steps: Develop analytics tracking that provides detailed information about the usage of the shortened URLs.
ai_assist.expected_ai_output: Draft implementation artifacts that require engineer review before acceptance.

tasks.engineer_validation: Confirm the task output is technically correct, scoped, and testable.

artifacts.api_contract: Include a specification or update to the API contract to support new endpoints for expiring links and analytics.
artifacts.task_summary: L1: LLM Task 1 | L2: LLM Task 2
test_plan: Create tests for expiring link functionality to ensure URLs expire as expected.
Develop test cases for analytics tracking to verify data accuracy.
artifacts.documentation: Update the user documentation to include information about new features and how they can be used.
artifacts.requirement: Include a detailed requirement document outlining the enhancements and their implementation plan.

checks: All core artifact sections are present.
Task dependency graph is internally consistent.
risks: Test plan may be insufficient for broad behavioral coverage.
AI-assisted execution traceability is weak; add notes on prompt and review decisions.
AI-generated tasks can miss hidden constraints unless manually reviewed.
Performance and abuse protection controls are not production-complete in this prototype.
